docs.title: Blueprint Image Exporter
docs.text:
Blueprint Image Exporter is an Unreal Engine 5 editor plugin for exporting high-quality PNG images from graph-based assets without relying on viewport screenshots.

It is designed for documentation, reviews, tutorials, changelogs, and any workflow where you need clean captures of Unreal graph content that can be shared outside the editor.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text:
• Exports graph images as PNG files.
  • Works from Unreal Editor tooling instead of external screen capture workflows.
  • Supports Blueprint-oriented editor workflows with batch export in mind.
  • Keeps output suitable for polished documentation and team communication.

Info

This documentation lives inside the plugin folder so the docs setup stays isolated from the rest of the repository.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Editor-only plugin architecture for Unreal Engine 5.
  • Source-compatible support starting at UE 5.0.0.
  • Built for graph capture workflows where readability matters.
  • Intended for professional documentation and asset review pipelines.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
The plugin is implemented as native Unreal Engine modules. A typical module declaration looks like this:

class FBlueprintImageExporterModule : public IModuleInterface
{
public:
    virtual void StartupModule() override;
    virtual void ShutdownModule() override;
};

docs.title: Why use it
docs.text:
If your team needs to publish Blueprint-related visuals to internal docs, release notes, or public knowledge bases, this plugin helps you produce repeatable image output that is easier to maintain than manual screenshots.

Continue to the setup guide for installation and local documentation workflow details.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text: This guide covers both plugin installation in Unreal Engine and local documentation generation with MkDocs Material.

docs.title: Install the plugin
docs.text:
1. Place the plugin under your project at Plugins/BlueprintImageExporter.
  2. Open the Unreal project in the editor.
  3. Enable the plugin from Edit -> Plugins if it is not already enabled.
  4. Restart the editor when prompted.

Warning

The plugin is editor-only. It is intended for authoring and export workflows inside Unreal Editor, not runtime packaging.

docs.title: Basic usage
docs.text:
1. Open Project Settings -> Plugins -> Blueprint Image Exporter.
  2. Configure export preferences such as output folder, padding, and background behavior.
  3. In the Content Browser, right-click supported assets and choose the export action.
  4. Select an output location for the generated PNG files.

docs.title: Build the documentation locally
docs.text:
The documentation source is stored inside the plugin folder so it can travel with the plugin.

cd Plugins/BlueprintImageExporter
python -m pip install --upgrade pip
python -m pip install mkdocs-material
mkdocs build

After the build finishes, the generated static site is available in Plugins/BlueprintImageExporter/site.

docs.title: Publish flow
docs.text:
The repository workflow builds docs from inside the plugin folder and pushes only the generated HTML output to the public docs repository.

That means:

  • The private plugin source stays in this repository.
  • The public repo receives only the built site contents.
  • Raw markdown files from docs/ are not deployed.
